images.caption:
Figure 1:All sources of randomness behind a generative model.Training and then sampling a generative model is a chain of pseudo-random
draws.
They fall into two lotteries. Thetraining lottery(left) is drawn from four sources: the random weight initialisation, the order in which examples are visited, the fresh Gaussian noise the flow-matching loss injects
at every gradient step, and the bitwise non-determinism
of multi-GPU execution. This draws a single trained network out of the many networks the same recipe could have produced. Thegeneration lottery(right) then draws a fresh initial noisexT∼𝒩​(0,I)x_{T}\!\sim\!\mathcal{N}(0,I)for every
sampled image. Common practice accounts for only, reporting error bars over sampling seeds on one fixed
network. In this work we study all five.

Figure 2:The FID lottery in SiT-B/2 at 400k steps.Each violin is one of2525independently trained SiT-B/2
models, sorted by per-seed mean Inception FID. Small dots are the250250individual sampling-seed evaluations and short black ticks are
per-seed means. The two highlighted markers pick out the single best
(33.59\mathbf{33.59}) and worst (35.69\mathbf{35.69}) FID across the panel,
a2.10\mathbf{2.10}-point gap produced purely by changing seeds. Each
violin is short (σwithin≈0.14\sigma_{\text{within}}\!\approx\!0.14, evaluation
lottery). The per-seed means stagger over a3×3{\times}wider range
(σbetween=0.44\sigma_{\text{between}}\!=\!0.44, training lottery).

Figure 3:Variance decomposition of the training-seed lottery
(SiT-B/2, 400k, no CFG).(a) Per-seed mean Inception FID under the three single-source
conditions plus the fully-stochastic baseline (vary all) and
thesame seedcontrol. Each dot is one training seed, boxes show
the 25/50/75 percentiles. (b) Between-seedσ\sigma(coral) versus
within-seed samplingσ\sigma(sage) per condition. The four
random-source conditions are ordered monotonically by between-seedσ\sigma,
with samplingσ\sigmaflat across all four. The rightmostsame seedcolumn is a control that fixes init, data order,andtraining noise to identical values, leaving only the bitwise
non-determinism of multi-GPU (DDP) execution: its between-seedσ\sigmacollapses to0.0470.047, below the within-seed sampling floor, even though
the trained weights differ by≈5{\approx}5–6%6\%of their norm.
Numerical non-determinism is thus not a meaningful source of FID
variance.

Figure 4:What does FID spectrum looks like?Each row is one scene rendered by SiT-XL model whose Inception FID
falls log-uniformly from𝟒𝟑\mathbf{43}(left) to3.6\mathbf{3.6}(right),
a𝟏𝟐×\mathbf{12\times}range: quality improves toward the right, as FID goes down.
FID is definedat the distribution level. It’s a Fréchet distance
between Gaussians fit to the Inception features of the reference distribution (the ImageNet dataset) and50,00050{,}000generated images. It is a property of the wholeset, never of any single generated image.
For a fuller, set-level impression
at each FID level, see the per-class galleries inAppendix G.

Figure 5:Per-cell guidance tuning halves the seed-induced FID
spread, but reshuffles which seeds rank best.Per-(training, sampling)-seed golden-section CFG search
(GS-FID) across2525SiT-B/2 training seeds (400400k steps,1010sampling seeds per cell). (a) Per-seed violins of guided Inception
FID, sorted by per-seed mean. The relative spread tightens toCoV=0.67%\mathrm{CoV}\!=\!0.67\%, about half the1.26%1.26\%measured unguided
on the same panel. (b) Tuning does not preserve the seed ranking:
unguided and guided ranks correlate at only Spearmanρ=0.73\rho\!=\!0.73. Lavender lines mark seeds that barely move
(|Δ​rank|<5|\Delta\!\operatorname{rank}|\!<\!5), coral lines the8/258/25seeds
that shift by|Δ​rank|≥5|\Delta\!\operatorname{rank}|\!\geq\!5.

Figure 6:The seed lottery across compute and model size.(a) Inception FID over training: thin pastel lines are
individual training-seed trajectories, bold lines are per-step means.
The spread between seeds stays wide at every checkpoint and does not
shrink as training converges. (b) Coefficient of
variationσ/μ\sigma/\muover training: all four models stay near a11–2%2\%band. Bigger models do not yield proportionally tighter FID.
(c) Spearmanρ\rhobetween the seed ranking at stepttand at22M:
weak before∼1\sim\!1M steps.

Figure 7:The luck of the draw: a1.2\boldsymbol{1.2}–2.0×\boldsymbol{2.0{\times}}convergence gap.For each model the dashed horizontal line marks the targetTT, the FID reached by theunluckiestof∼20\sim\!20seeds at22M. The green dot is the step at which theluckiestseed
first crossesTT. The coral dot sits at22M where the unlucky seed
finally reaches it. The amber band between them is the training
compute the unlucky seed wastes catching up. The per-step shaded band is
the min–max envelope across seeds, and the bold line is the per-step mean.

Figure 8:𝝁\boldsymbol{\mu}P-coordinated LR sweep at𝟏𝟎𝟎\boldsymbol{100}k for SiT-S/B/L/XL.Solid lines are the per-LR mean Inception FID across1010training seeds, the shaded envelope is the per-LR seed min–max, and the
open ring on each curve circles each size’s best-mean-FID dot.
(a) Unguided FID is monotone in LR for every size, so the highlighted
dot sits at5×10−45\!\times\!10^{-4}(the edge of training stability) for
all four. (b) GS-FID has flat-bottomed valleys aroundω⋆≈2\omega^{\star}\!\approx\!2–3×10−43\!\times\!10^{-4}. Within seed noise,
two LRs on either side are indistinguishable from the highlighted dot,
giving a1.7×1.7{\times}LR window per size. (c) Between-seedσbetween/μ\sigma_{\text{between}}/\muon log scale. The green band is the11–2%2\%floor ofSec. 4.4. Open rings mark
each size’s optimum LR from (b): the CoV doesnotdip there.

Figure 9:The FID lottery, drawn as two slot machines.A casino-themed rendering of the same two lotteries diagrammed from first
principles inFigure 1. Reporting an FID means
pulling two levers. Thetraining lottery(left) draws one network
from three coupled sources of randomness. These are the random weight
initialisation (a die), the shuffled data order (a deck), and the
per-step flow-matching noise (a trace), and they yield one trained network
(centre, tagged by its seed). Thegeneration lottery(right) then draws1010sampling seeds and scores1010FIDs ofthatnetwork. On the measured SiT-B/2 panel
(Figure 2) resampling a fixed network barely moves the
score (σwithin≈0.14\sigma_{\mathrm{within}}\!\approx\!0.14), while retraining moves
it3.2×\mathbf{3.2\times}farther (σbetween=0.44\sigma_{\mathrm{between}}\!=\!0.44): a2.10\mathbf{2.10}-point gap between the best (33.59\mathbf{33.59}) and worst
(35.69\mathbf{35.69}) evaluation, from seeds alone.

header: Appendix DAdditional analyses on Inception FID

images.caption:
Figure 10:(a) Vary all(σbetween=0.438\sigma_{\text{between}}\!=\!0.438).
All three randomness sources are free. The panel is the same data asFigure 2, repeated here as the reference baseline for
the three single-source panels that follow.

Figure 11:(b) Vary noise(σbetween=0.336\sigma_{\text{between}}\!=\!0.336).
Init and data order are fixed. Only the per-step Gaussian noise of
the flow-matching loss varies between training runs. Noise alone
reproduces≈77%{\approx}77\%of the baseline between-seedσ\sigmaof (a).

Figure 12:(c) Vary init(σbetween=0.294\sigma_{\text{between}}\!=\!0.294).
Data order and noise are fixed. Only the parameter initialisation
varies. Init alone reproduces≈67%{\approx}67\%of the baseline
between-seedσ\sigmaof (a).

Figure 13:(d) Vary data(σbetween=0.221\sigma_{\text{between}}\!=\!0.221).
Init and noise are fixed. Only the data-loader order varies. Data
order alone reproduces≈51%{\approx}51\%of the baseline between-seedσ\sigmaof (a). All four panels share the same y-range and a
consistent layout: every violin is one training seed showing the
Gaussian KDE of its1010sampling-seed evaluations, the small dots
are individual evaluations, and the black tick is the per-seed mean.

Figure 14:Rank stability of the 25 training seeds (SiT-B/2, 400k).(a) Bump chart: each line is one training seed traced through three
ranking criteria — mean, min, and max FID across its 10 sampling seeds.
Crossings dominate the picture: the best seed bymeanis rarely
the best byminormax. Coral and teal highlight the
seeds that are best- and worst-by-mean to make their rank trajectories
visible.
(b) Spearmanρ\rhobetween rankings under all six combinations of{\{Inception, DINOv2}×{\}\times\{mean, min, max}\}. Inception and
DINOv2 agree strongly on themeanranking (ρ=0.99\rho\!=\!0.99) but
the agreement weakens for themaxcriterion (ρ=0.94\rho\!=\!0.94),
i.e. the two metrics disagree more often on which seed had the
worst-case sampling.

Figure 15:Seed optimality: are “good” init seeds universal?
(SiT-B/2, 400k, no CFG.)(a) Heatmap of mean Inception FID over a10×1510\!\times\!15grid of
init seeds (rows) and (data, noise) pairings (columns). Cells span∼33.9{\sim}33.9–35.935.9. A single init does not consistently shade greenest
across rows.
(b) Bump chart of the same data: each line is one init seed traced
through its rank within each (data, noise) pair. Heavy crossings make
the rank instability visceral. Kendall’sW=0.41W\!=\!0.41on the init
rankings (mean criterion) shows significant agreement, but far from
concordance. The “best” init by grand mean wins only5/155/15pairs.

Figure 16:Seed-induced𝟗𝟓%\boldsymbol{95\%}confidence interval
as a function of the reported Inception FID.For a mean FID computed
fromNNindependently trained models withK=10K\!=\!10sampling seeds
each, the normal-approximation half-width isCI95=1.96​CoV​F/N\mathrm{CI}_{95}\!=\!1.96\,\mathrm{CoV}\,F/\sqrt{N}, whereCoV=σbetween/μ\mathrm{CoV}\!=\!\sigma_{\text{between}}/\muis the scale-invariant
noise floor reported for Inception FID across the7676(model,step)(\text{model},\text{step})cells of the scaling sweep
(Table 9, with median1.30%1.30\%andp10−p90=0.88−1.73%p_{10}\!-\!p_{90}\!=\!0.88\!-\!1.73\%).
The solid coral curve uses the median CoV. The shaded band traces thep10−p90p_{10}\!-\!p_{90}envelope atN=1N\!=\!1. Three readings: atFID≈15\text{FID}\!\approx\!15(typical converged SiT-L), a single trained
model carries a±0.38\pm 0.38FID seed-only CI – four times the±0.1\pm 0.1gaps that routinely separate published methods. Pulling that
CI under±0.1\pm 0.1requiresN=25N\!=\!25training seeds. Within-seed
(sampling-only) contributions are absorbed into the displayed band:
atK=10K\!=\!10the within-CoV is≈0.4%\!\approx\!0.4\%on this panel and
contributes less than5%5\%to the total variance of the seed-mean.

header: Appendix ETheory of golden-section search for FID(CFG)

images.caption:
Figure 17:Golden-section search on𝐅𝐈𝐃​(ω)\boldsymbol{\mathrm{FID}(\omega)}(referenced briefly fromSec. 4.3).(a)Figure 17: pseudocode for the bracket-contraction loop
(the bracket-contraction sequence itself is illustrated inFigure 18).
(b) Two interior probesx1=b−ρ​(b−a)x_{1}=b-\rho(b-a)andx2=a+ρ​(b−a)x_{2}=a+\rho(b-a)withρ=1/φ≈0.618\rho=1/\varphi\!\approx\!0.618split
the bracket[a,b][a,b]. The side with the largerff-value is
discarded.

Figure 18:Bracket contraction across iterations ofFigure 17.Companion toFigure 17. Kept half
(lavender) and discarded half (faded coral) of the bracket on the
illustrative interval[a0,b0]=[0.3,1.9][a_{0},b_{0}]=[0.3,1.9]. The surviving probe
of each iteration is reused as one of the two probes for the next
iteration, so each step past the initial pair costs exactly one new
FID evaluation. The bracket length contracts by a factorρ\rhoper
step.

header: Appendix FReplication across DINOv2 FID and Inception PRDC

images.caption:
Figure 19:The four PRDC metrics on a 2D toy.Marker convention:circles (∙\bullet) arerealpoints
and triangles (▲\blacktriangle) aregeneratedpoints. The
larger marker in each panel is the entity the metric tests. In panels
(a, c, d) the metric is a per-point indicator, drawn green
(∙\bullet/▲\blacktriangle= counted /
covered) or red and hollow
(∘\circ/△\triangle= not counted /
uncovered). Lavender (▲{\color[rgb]{0.484375,0.43359375,0.66015625}\definecolor[named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0.484375,0.43359375,0.66015625}\blacktriangle}) marks
generated points whose coverage status is not relevant in that panel.
Coral discs are real-anchoredkk-NN balls and lavender discs are
generated-anchored balls. The amber disc in (d) is the outlier
generated point’s enlarged ball.Scenario:reals sit on a curved manifold plus an isolated
mini-mode of three tightly grouped points in the upper-left. The
generated points cover the manifold but miss the isolated mode and
add one outlier far above.
(a)Precision=7/87/8: each generated point is tested against
the union of real-anchored balls, and the outlier triangle is the only
hollow one.
(b)Density=1.001.00: same balls, but each generated point is
labelled with the count of real-balls it lies in (a multiplicity, not
an indicator). Cluster points score11–33, while the outlier scores0.
(c)Coverage=7/107/10: each real point is tested against the
same real-anchored balls. The three isolated reals have tinykk-NN
balls (ρkR≈0.2\rho_{k}^{R}\!\approx\!0.2) that no generated point reaches,
so they appear as red hollow circles.
(d)Recall=7/107/10: real points are tested against
generated-anchored balls instead. The outlier generated point’s ball
(ρkG=3.75\rho_{k}^{G}\!=\!3.75, amber) is1.8×1.8{\times}larger than any
cluster ball and engulfs much of the manifold. The three isolated
reals remain hollow, so recall coincides with coverageon
this draw, butby a different mechanism: in (c) the balls
are fixed across draws ofGG, while in (d) every sampling seed
redraws the entire ball geometry, inflating the within-seed
estimator floor reported inTable 5.

Figure 20:Seed lottery on the same SiT-B/2 panel under DINOv2
FID.Each violin is one of2525trained models, sorted left-to-right
by per-seed mean. Violin shape traces the within-seed sampling
distribution. The black tick is the per-seed mean. The training-seed
mean ticks span694.8694.8to718.8718.8around grand mean708.6708.6. The
between-to-within ratio isσbetween/σwithin=4.82×\sigma_{\text{between}}/\sigma_{\text{within}}\!=\!4.82{\times}, against3.19×3.19{\times}for Inception FID, so the staircase of mean ticks
stretches over a wider multiple of the violin height than inFigure 2.

Figure 21:Seed lottery under Inception Precision.Y-axis values
are multiplied by100100for readability. Per-seed means span0.4800.480to0.4910.491around grand mean0.4850.485. Violin height (within-seed sampling
spread) and the staircase of mean ticks (between-seed training spread)
are comparable, sinceσbetween/σwithin=1.14×\sigma_{\text{between}}/\sigma_{\text{within}}\!=\!1.14{\times}. A
multi-sampling-seed CI therefore covers roughly half of the seed-induced
envelope on precision, in contrast with FID where it covers a quarter.

Figure 22:Seed lottery under Inception Recall (the inversion
case).Y-axis values are multiplied by100100for readability. Per-seed
means span0.3120.312to0.3170.317around grand mean0.3140.314. Each violin is
taller than the staircase of mean ticks:σbetween/σwithin=0.28×\sigma_{\text{between}}/\sigma_{\text{within}}\!=\!0.28{\times}, an
inversion of the FID asymmetry. On Inception recall the right CI to
report on a fixed trained model is the sampling-only one. This is the
opposite recommendation from FID and from the other PRDC metrics
(Table 6).

Figure 23:Coefficient of variation across compute and scale, for
six metrics.Each panel plots between-seedCoV=σ/μ\mathrm{CoV}\!=\!\sigma/\muversus training step on the
variance-over-training panel for SiT-S/B/L/XL with the clean seed sets
ofTable 2. The shaded green stripe in each panel
is that metric’s empirical[p10,p90][p_{10},p_{90}]envelope of CoV across
its7676(model,step)(\mathrm{model},\mathrm{step})cells, reported in the panel
title. Inception FID’s envelope is0.880.88–1.73%1.73\%, matching the11–2%2\%headline ofFigure 6b. DINOv2 FID
(0.560.56–1.50%1.50\%) and Inception precision/coverage
(0.280.28–0.70%0.70\%,0.200.20–1.04%1.04\%) sit below the FID envelope at
every model size. Recall (0.380.38–1.56%1.56\%) and density
(0.540.54–1.26%1.26\%) fall in between. Each metric therefore has its own
scale-invariant relative noise floor. The floor is wider for recall
becauseσ\sigmadoes not shrink with compute on SiT-B/L
(Table 9).

Figure 24:Rank stability across compute, for six metrics.Each
panel plots Spearmanρ\rhobetween the seed ranking at training stepttand at22M, one curve per model size, mirroring the layout ofFigure 6c. Dashed horizontal lines markρ=0.8\rho\!=\!0.8(the high-stability target) andρ=0\rho\!=\!0(random).
Inception FID, DINOv2 FID and Inception density approachρ≈0.8\rho\!\approx\!0.8by∼1.4\sim\!1.4M on most models. Inception
precision and coverage stabilise more slowly. Inception Recall is the
outlier:ρ\rhostays below0.60.6at every pre-final checkpoint on
every model and dips negative early in training, so the recall
ranking at any intermediate checkpoint is essentially uninformative
about the eventual recall ranking. The recall panel uses a wideryy-range[−0.4,1.05][-0.4,1.05]to accommodate the dip below zero.
